title: Source & API Mapping Layer (API-agnostic core + EODHD primary)
description: Separates the report schema from any specific vendor. Each schema field path maps to a PRIMARY source (EODHD where it fits your student-rate Fundamentals plan) and one or more FALLBACK sources (free). If you ever swap EODHD for FMP/Tiingo/etc, you only edit this file, never the report schema. 'eodhd_endpoint' values reference EODHD's documented endpoints; agents using EODHD's MCP server (75 tools) can also call by tool name. Field paths use dot/[] notation into report_schema.json.
swap_instructions: To change provider: replace 'eodhd_endpoint' + 'eodhd_path' on each row with the new vendor's locator. Report schema is untouched.

global_notes.currency_normalization: EODHD returns native reporting currency. Agent must convert market_cap/EV/revenue to USD_m using a dated FX (EODHD forex endpoint or ECB reference rate, both acceptable T2/T5) and record the rate+date in datapoint.note.
global_notes.liquidity: avg_daily_value_traded computed by agent: EODHD EOD volume x close, 90-day average. data_quality B; mark T6 for the computation.
global_notes.tier_assignment_rule: When a fact comes from EODHD but EODHD itself sourced it from a filing (US via SEC, Canada via SEDAR), agent should record source_tier as T5_api_aggregator UNLESS it has independently opened the underlying filing, in which case T1 with the filing named. Do not inflate tiers.

mappings.note: EODHD description is often generic; agent should rewrite from the latest annual report for accuracy.
Not in any API. Agent-derived from business description + supply-chain reasoning.

fallback: compute: mktcap + net debt + minorities - cash (T6)

mappings.note: For small caps EODHD segment data is thin; go to the filing.
Often a required risk-factor disclosure; agent should grep filings.

fallback: NI 43-101 / JORC technical report (T1) — MANDATORY source
mappings.note: Never take reserves from an aggregator. Always the technical report.
Genuine data gap at this budget; use free proxies, mark quality C/D.
Maturity schedule is in the filing notes, not the API.

mappings.eodhd_path: use Screener API to find same-sector small caps, then pull fundamentals each
mappings.note: EODHD Screener filters by market cap, sector, exchange — useful for peer discovery AND for the upstream screening funnel.
EODHD insider feed is strong for US; for non-US go to the local regulator filing.
EODHD news is a discovery aid, not a citable authority for the tailwind itself — anchor to T2/T3.

fallback: agent meta-record of how the candidate was reached (T6)
mappings.note: Pipeline metadata, not an external fact. The coordinator agent records which discovery route produced the name.

fallback: agent inference from the connection map (T6)

mappings.eodhd_path: AnalystRatings / Earnings estimate provider count (where present)
fallback: count distinct brokers in any estimate feed (T5)
mappings.note: Thin or absent for small caps — absence itself is the signal (under-covered).
Low volume + a real thesis = language-barrier edge.

fallback: inspect the primary filing on the local regulator (T1/T2)
mappings.note: Non-English primary disclosure is a structural coverage barrier the agent can arbitrage via translation.

fallback: agent judgement: does the tag match what they actually do? (T6)
mappings.note: Mis-tagged names are invisible to thematic screens — a discovery gold-seam.

fallback: see source_taxonomy.discovery_feeds_event_triggers — permits, USAspending/EU TED awards, UN Comtrade flow shifts, export-control notices, Google Patents clusters, refinancing filings (T1/T2)
mappings.note: Most trigger types are NOT in any fundamentals API — they live in regulator/procurement/registry feeds. This is the point: the edge is in feeds others don't read.

fallback: date stamped on the originating filing/award/notice (T1/T2)
